cases.prompt: Production checkout deploy is blocked because the Stripe production secret is unavailable. Create the blocker in the storefront repo; it belongs in the production secret manager. The release cannot continue until it is set, then rerun ./smoke-checkout.

Domain verification is blocked: the team lacks DNS provider access to add the required TXT record. Open a high-priority HITL issue in the web repo. The domain cannot launch until the record exists; add it in the DNS provider and verify with dig TXT example.com.

The iOS release is blocked pending Apple App Store approval. Create a high-priority HITL blocker in the mobile repo. A release owner must approve it in App Store Connect; publishing cannot proceed until then. Verify by checking the App Store Connect release status.

expected_outcome.verification: check the App Store Connect release status

cases.prompt: Migration deployment is blocked because the deploy service account lacks Cloud SQL permissions. Create the blocker in the API repo. A cloud administrator must grant the permission in IAM; the migration cannot run otherwise. Verify with ./deploy-migrations --dry-run.

Transactional email sending is blocked because the vendor billing account is suspended. Create a high-priority HITL issue in the notifications repo. The billing owner must restore the account in the vendor billing portal; email cannot be sent until it is active. Verify with ./send-email-smoke.

The payment webhook secret is missing and deployment is blocked. HITL issue #91 already requests the release owner to set it in the production secret manager; do not create a duplicate. The release remains blocked until then. Verify with ./verify-webhook.

A production API credential is unavailable, blocking launch. Create a high-priority HITL issue in the service repo. The owner must add it in the production secret manager; do not expose its value. Verify with ./healthcheck.
The date parser returns local time instead of UTC. Fix it and run its unit tests.
The payment provider is currently down according to its status page. Retry after it recovers and report the incident status.
Correct a typo in the deployment README and run markdownlint.
